label: MARK × KAREN · ON TRACK · HGV reapplication and Ban-the-Box hire
framing: Mark, 58, ex-offender, former HGV driver. Karen Mensah is direct, no hoops, lays out the Ban-the-Box pathway and HGV licence reapplication.

lines.speaker: adviser
lines.text: Mark, it's Karen. Quick one for you. Bardon Aggregates have come back. They want you for the yard role with the HGV class C when your licence is reinstated. Twelve-month contract.

lines.speaker: adviser
lines.text: They do. Bardon is a Ban-the-Box employer. Your conviction was on the application. They've already taken the decision. The yard supervisor is named Ravi, he wants to meet you next Tuesday.

lines.speaker: adviser
lines.text: DVLA have your reapplication. Class C medical is booked for Thursday at the Lincoln Centre. Restart pays for it. After the medical it's about six weeks for the licence to come back.

lines.speaker: customer
lines.text: So I take the yard work first, then I'm back on HGV when the licence is back. That's the deal.

lines.speaker: adviser
lines.text: That's exactly the deal. The yard is forty hours, twenty-six thousand a year. When you move to HGV in six weeks the rate goes up to thirty-one. Bardon have agreed it in writing.

lines.speaker: customer
lines.text: Good. Send me the appointment details for Tuesday and I will be there. None of the run-around.

lines.speaker: adviser
lines.text: No run-around. Email is in your inbox. One last thing. Universal Credit tapers off as your wages come in, not all at once. We'll do the better-off-in-work numbers with you before the Bardon start date. So you know.

lines.speaker: customer
lines.text: Sensible. Thanks Karen. I'll see you Tuesday.
